NeuralOS is a neural generative model that simulates an entire operating system. query: how do I interact with it
text: To interact with NeuralOS, move your mouse inside the interaction box and the model generates the next frame in response to where your cursor goes. Click (left-click or right-click) to perform click actions, and use your keyboard to type - just like a real desktop. The page controls allow you to set the number of sampling (diffusion) steps and toggle options such as "Use RNN" and "Auto Input".

query: how does it deal with the mouse cursor
text: NeuralOS encodes the cursor's (x, y) position as a Gaussian spatial map (an extra input channel), which cut cursor error from about 130 pixels to about 1.6 pixels. It uses the mouse movement, click, or keypress to predict the next frame, then feeds that frame back as input.

ProgramAsWeights is a million-scale dataset of real user-chatbot conversations for studying how people actually use LLMs. The dataset is available on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yuntian-deng/ProgramAsWeights-4.8M-Full).

NeuralOS uses an RNN to track the operating-system state and a diffusion renderer to generate each screen frame. It is autoregressive, so it predicts the next frame from past frames and the user's input, then feeds that frame back as input. The cursor position was a challenge, but encoding it as a Gaussian spatial map cut cursor error from about 130 pixels to about 1.6 pixels.

text: Yuntian's work centers on improving LLMs with explicit, reusable instructions, and building safer, more general models. His projects include [ProgramAsWeights](https://programasweights.com), [NeuralOS](https://neural-os.com), and [WildChat](https://wildvisualizer.com).

Yuntian's work centers on improving LLMs with explicit, reusable instructions, and building safer, more general models. His projects include [ProgramAsWeights](https://programasweights.com), [NeuralOS](https://neural-os.com), and [WildChat](https://wildvisualizer.com).

Yuntian's work centers on improving LLMs with explicit, reusable instructions, and building safer, more general models. His projects include [ProgramAsWeights](https://programasweights.com), [NeuralOS](https://neural-os.com), and [WildChat](https://wildvisualizer.com).

Yuntian is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at the University of Waterloo. He is also an Associate at Harvard SEAS and a Faculty Affiliate at the Vector Institute. He is Co-Chief Technical Officer (Co-CTO) of ACL Rolling Review (ARR). He was a Visiting Professor at NVIDIA (Yejin Choi's team) from November 2024 to November 2025. He is based at the University of Waterloo in Waterloo, Ontario, Canada.

You are looking at NeuralOS, a neural generative model that simulates an entire operating system. Every screen pixel is generated by a neural network, with no real kernel, no installed applications, and no fixed menus or icons.

NeuralOS uses an RNN that tracks the operating-system state (like a kernel) together with a diffusion renderer that generates each screen frame, like a desktop manager. It is autoregressive: given past frames and the user's input, it predicts the next frame, then feeds that frame back as input - responding to every user action, indefinitely, rather than playing a fixed clip.

query: what compilers does program as weights offer
text: ProgramAsWeights (PAW) offers a Standard compiler (paw-4b-qwen3-0.6b), a Compact compiler (paw-4b-gpt2), and a Finetuned Standard compiler (paw-ft-bs48). The Compact compiler is smaller and runs in the browser via WebAssembly. The Finetuned Standard compiler is the highest-accuracy option and finetunes a per-spec LoRA.

query: what is implicit chain of thought
text: Implicit chain of thought is an implicit understanding that an AI does not explicitly state that it is generating a thought by analogy. It is a key weakness of many LLMs and is a central idea in ProgramAsWeights.
